# ============================================================
# RULES PAGE PROMPTS — Frontend + Backend
# 4 prompts total:
#   R-F1: Add Rules to existing Sidebar.tsx only
#   R-F2: Rules.tsx — idle state (rule list + run panel)
#   R-F3: Rules.tsx — processing + results states
#   R-B1: Backend router + service
#
# SOURCE: code.html generated by Stitch
# The main content layout (col-span-8 rules list + col-span-4
# run panel) is taken DIRECTLY from Stitch HTML.
# Sidebar and Header are NOT touched.
# ============================================================


# ============================================================
# R-F1 — Add Rules nav item to existing Sidebar.tsx only
# ============================================================

Open src/components/Layout/Sidebar.tsx.

Find the NAV_ITEMS array. It currently looks like this:
  const NAV_ITEMS = [
    { label: 'Mailbox',         icon: 'mail',        to: '/mailbox'       },
    { label: 'New Task',        icon: 'add_task',    to: '/new-task'      },
    { label: 'All Tasks',       icon: 'list_alt',    to: '/tasks'         },
    { label: 'Scheduled Task',  icon: 'schedule',    to: '/scheduled'     },
    { label: 'Import/Export',   icon: 'swap_horiz',  to: '/import-export' },
    { label: 'Logs',            icon: 'terminal',    to: '/logs'          },
  ]

Add ONE new item between Import/Export and Logs:
  { label: 'Rules', icon: 'rule', to: '/rules' }

The array after the change:
  const NAV_ITEMS = [
    { label: 'Mailbox',         icon: 'mail',        to: '/mailbox'       },
    { label: 'New Task',        icon: 'add_task',    to: '/new-task'      },
    { label: 'All Tasks',       icon: 'list_alt',    to: '/tasks'         },
    { label: 'Scheduled Task',  icon: 'schedule',    to: '/scheduled'     },
    { label: 'Import/Export',   icon: 'swap_horiz',  to: '/import-export' },
    { label: 'Rules',           icon: 'rule',        to: '/rules'         },
    { label: 'Logs',            icon: 'terminal',    to: '/logs'          },
  ]

That is the ONLY change to Sidebar.tsx.
Do not touch the JSX, styling, active state logic, or anything else.

Then open src/App.tsx.
Add the /rules route alongside the existing routes:
  import Rules from './pages/Rules'
  <Route path="/rules" element={<Rules />} />

Then create src/pages/Rules.tsx with a placeholder:
  export default function Rules() {
    return <div className="p-8 text-on-surface">Rules — coming soon</div>
  }

Run npm run dev and confirm:
  ✓ "Rules" appears in the sidebar between Import/Export and Logs
  ✓ The icon is "rule" (material symbol)
  ✓ Clicking /rules highlights it with the active state
    (white text + border-l-4 border-[#4F6BFF] + bg-[#4F6BFF]/10)
  ✓ All other sidebar items are unchanged
  ✓ Sidebar background, font, logo, and settings link unchanged


# ============================================================
# R-F2 — Rules.tsx: idle state (rule list + run panel)
# ============================================================

Open src/pages/Rules.tsx and replace it entirely.

This page uses the existing Header component with title="Rules".
The layout is AppLayout's flex column wrapper — same as all pages.

Root: <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col overflow-hidden">

--- TYPES (top of file, before component) ---

```tsx
type RuleStatus = 'idle' | 'processing' | 'done'

interface Rule {
  id: string
  name: string
  instruction: string     // the plain English sentence
  enabled: boolean
  matchCount?: number     // "X matches this week"
}

interface RunResult {
  emailId: string
  subject: string
  from: string
  matchedRuleName: string
  matchedRuleId: string
  folder: string
  confidence: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'
  reasoning: string
  status: 'moved' | 'no_match' | 'error'
  errorMessage?: string
}
```

--- STATE ---

```tsx
const [rules, setRules] = useState<Rule[]>([
  {
    id: '1', name: 'SABS Team', enabled: true, matchCount: 42,
    instruction: "If emails are from anyone in the SABS team (Sarah, Amir, Ben, or Sophie), move them to the SABS Team folder.",
  },
  {
    id: '2', name: 'Pending Action', enabled: true, matchCount: 15,
    instruction: "If an email requires me to take any action, complete a task, or respond urgently, move it to the Pending Action folder.",
  },
  {
    id: '3', name: 'Newsletters', enabled: true, matchCount: 128,
    instruction: "If the email is a newsletter, marketing message, or promotional content, move it to the Newsletters folder.",
  },
  {
    id: '4', name: 'Finance Invoices', enabled: false, matchCount: 0,
    instruction: "If the email contains an invoice, payment confirmation, or receipt, move it to Finance/Invoices.",
  },
])

const [runStatus, setRunStatus] = useState<RuleStatus>('idle')
const [startDate, setStartDate] = useState('2025-05-01')
const [endDate, setEndDate]     = useState('2025-05-14')
const [results, setResults]     = useState<RunResult[]>([])
const [showAddForm, setShowAddForm]   = useState(false)
const [editingRule, setEditingRule]   = useState<Rule | null>(null)
const [showDeleteConfirm, setShowDeleteConfirm] = useState<string | null>(null)
const [showReasoningFor, setShowReasoningFor]   = useState<string | null>(null)
const [resultFilter, setResultFilter] = useState<'all'|'moved'|'no_match'|'error'>('all')
const [resultSearch, setResultSearch] = useState('')
```

--- MAIN LAYOUT ---

```tsx
<main className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-lg bg-surface">
  <div className="max-w-6xl mx-auto space-y-lg">

    {/* Block 1 — Page title row (from Stitch HTML exactly) */}
    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
      <div>
        <h2 className="font-page-title text-page-title text-on-surface">Rules</h2>
        <p className="text-on-surface-variant mt-1">
          Write plain English rules. When run, all rules are sent to an
          AI together with your emails.
        </p>
      </div>
      <button
        onClick={() => setShowAddForm(true)}
        className="bg-primary text-white px-md py-sm rounded-lg flex items-center gap-2 hover:bg-primary-container transition-all shadow-md"
      >
        <span className="material-symbols-outlined">add</span>
        <span className="font-medium">Add Rule</span>
      </button>
    </div>

    {/* 12-col grid — from Stitch HTML exactly */}
    <div className="grid grid-cols-12 gap-gutter">

      {/* Left col: rules list */}
      <div className="col-span-8 space-y-lg">
        {/* Rules card — see below */}
      </div>

      {/* Right col: run panel + results */}
      <div className="col-span-4 space-y-lg">
        {/* Run panel — see below */}
        {/* Results area — see below */}
      </div>

    </div>
  </div>
</main>
```

--- LEFT COLUMN: RULES CARD ---

Copy this structure DIRECTLY from Stitch HTML:

```tsx
<section className="bg-surface-container-lowest rounded-xl border border-outline-variant p-lg shadow-sm">

  {/* Card header */}
  <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-lg">
    <h3 className="font-section-label text-section-label uppercase text-outline">
      Your Rules
    </h3>
    <span className="text-meta text-outline">
      {rules.filter(r => r.enabled).length} active rules
    </span>
  </div>

  {/* Add rule inline form — shown when showAddForm=true */}
  {showAddForm && (
    <div className="mb-md p-md bg-surface-container-low rounded-lg border border-outline-variant space-y-3">
      <div>
        <label className="block text-meta text-on-surface-variant mb-1 font-medium">
          Rule name
        </label>
        <input
          type="text"
          placeholder="e.g. SABS Team, Pending Action..."
          className="w-full h-9 rounded-lg border border-outline-variant bg-white px-3 text-body focus:border-primary focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/20 focus:outline-none transition-all"
        />
      </div>
      <div>
        <label className="block text-meta text-on-surface-variant mb-1 font-medium">
          Rule instruction (plain English)
        </label>
        <textarea
          rows={3}
          placeholder='e.g. "If emails are from the SABS team, move to SABS Team folder."'
          className="w-full rounded-lg border border-outline-variant bg-white px-3 py-2 text-body focus:border-primary focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/20 focus:outline-none transition-all resize-none"
        />
        <p className="text-meta text-outline mt-1">
          Write naturally — the AI will interpret your instruction
        </p>
      </div>
      <div className="flex gap-2 justify-end">
        <button
          onClick={() => setShowAddForm(false)}
          className="h-9 px-4 border border-outline-variant bg-white text-on-surface-variant rounded-lg text-body font-medium hover:bg-surface-container transition-colors"
        >
          Cancel
        </button>
        <button
          className="h-9 px-4 bg-primary text-on-primary rounded-lg text-body font-medium hover:bg-primary-container transition-colors shadow-sm"
        >
          Save Rule
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  )}

  {/* Rule rows */}
  <div className="space-y-md">
    {rules.map((rule, index) => (
      <div
        key={rule.id}
        className={`flex items-start gap-md p-md bg-surface-container-low rounded-lg border border-transparent hover:border-outline-variant transition-all group ${!rule.enabled ? 'opacity-60' : ''}`}
      >
        {/* Number circle — from Stitch HTML */}
        <div className={`flex-shrink-0 w-8 h-8 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center font-bold text-sm mt-0.5 ${
          rule.enabled ? 'bg-primary/10 text-primary' : 'bg-gray-200 text-outline'
        }`}>
          {index + 1}
        </div>

        {/* Rule content */}
        <div className="flex-grow min-w-0">

          {/* Rule name */}
          <p className="text-body font-semibold text-on-surface mb-1">
            {rule.name}
          </p>

          {/* Instruction block — from Stitch: mono font inside white rounded border */}
          <div className="px-3 py-2 bg-white rounded border border-outline-variant font-mono text-xs mb-2 leading-relaxed">
            {rule.instruction}
          </div>

          {/* Meta row — from Stitch */}
          <div className="flex items-center gap-4">
            {rule.enabled ? (
              <span className="flex items-center gap-1 text-meta text-on-surface-variant">
                <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-[14px]">visibility</span>
                {rule.matchCount ?? 0} matches this week
              </span>
            ) : (
              <span className="flex items-center gap-1 text-meta text-on-surface-variant">
                <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-[14px]">block</span>
                Disabled
              </span>
            )}
          </div>
        </div>

        {/* Actions — hover-reveal from Stitch */}
        <div className={`flex items-center gap-2 shrink-0 ${rule.enabled ? 'opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100' : ''} transition-opacity`}>

          {/* Toggle */}
          <div className="flex items-center mr-2">
            <div
              onClick={() => setRules(prev =>
                prev.map(r => r.id === rule.id ? { ...r, enabled: !r.enabled } : r)
              )}
              className={`w-10 h-5 rounded-full relative cursor-pointer transition-colors ${
                rule.enabled ? 'bg-primary' : 'bg-gray-300'
              }`}
            >
              <div className={`absolute top-1 w-3 h-3 bg-white rounded-full shadow-sm transition-all ${
                rule.enabled ? 'right-1' : 'left-1'
              }`} />
            </div>
          </div>

          {/* Edit */}
          <button
            onClick={() => setEditingRule(rule)}
            className="p-2 text-outline hover:text-primary transition-colors rounded"
          >
            <span className="material-symbols-outlined">edit</span>
          </button>

          {/* Delete */}
          <button
            onClick={() => setShowDeleteConfirm(rule.id)}
            className="p-2 text-outline hover:text-error transition-colors rounded"
          >
            <span className="material-symbols-outlined">delete</span>
          </button>
        </div>
      </div>
    ))}
  </div>
</section>
```

--- RIGHT COLUMN: RUN PANEL ---

Copy DIRECTLY from Stitch HTML:

```tsx
<section className="bg-surface-container-highest rounded-xl border border-outline-variant p-lg shadow-sm">

  {/* Panel header */}
  <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mb-md">
    <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-primary"
      style={{ fontVariationSettings: "'FILL' 1" }}>
      auto_awesome
    </span>
    <h3 className="font-section-label text-section-label uppercase text-on-surface">
      Run Rules
    </h3>
  </div>

  <p className="text-meta text-on-surface-variant mb-md">
    Manually trigger rule analysis on a specific date range.
  </p>

  {/* Active rules info strip */}
  <div className="bg-white rounded-lg border border-outline-variant px-3 py-2 mb-md flex items-center gap-2">
    <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-primary text-[16px]"
      style={{ fontVariationSettings: "'FILL' 1" }}>
      info
    </span>
    <p className="text-meta text-on-surface-variant">
      <span className="font-semibold text-on-surface">
        {rules.filter(r => r.enabled).length} active rules
      </span>
      {' '}will run
      {rules.some(r => !r.enabled) && (
        <span className="text-outline">
          {' '}· {rules.filter(r => !r.enabled).length} disabled will be skipped
        </span>
      )}
    </p>
  </div>

  <div className="space-y-md">
    {/* Start date */}
    <div>
      <label className="block text-meta text-on-surface-variant mb-1 font-medium">
        Start Date
      </label>
      <div className="relative">
        <span className="material-symbols-outlined absolute left-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 text-outline text-[18px]">
          calendar_today
        </span>
        <input
          type="date"
          value={startDate}
          onChange={e => setStartDate(e.target.value)}
          disabled={runStatus === 'processing'}
          className="w-full bg-white border border-outline-variant rounded-lg pl-10 pr-4 py-2 text-body focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary focus:border-primary outline-none transition-all disabled:opacity-50"
        />
      </div>
    </div>

    {/* End date */}
    <div>
      <label className="block text-meta text-on-surface-variant mb-1 font-medium">
        End Date
      </label>
      <div className="relative">
        <span className="material-symbols-outlined absolute left-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 text-outline text-[18px]">
          calendar_today
        </span>
        <input
          type="date"
          value={endDate}
          onChange={e => setEndDate(e.target.value)}
          disabled={runStatus === 'processing'}
          className="w-full bg-white border border-outline-variant rounded-lg pl-10 pr-4 py-2 text-body focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary focus:border-primary outline-none transition-all disabled:opacity-50"
        />
      </div>
    </div>

    {/* Run / Cancel button */}
    {runStatus === 'processing' ? (
      <button
        onClick={() => setRunStatus('idle')}
        className="w-full border border-outline-variant bg-white text-on-surface-variant py-3 rounded-lg flex items-center justify-center gap-2 font-semibold hover:bg-surface-container transition-all"
      >
        <span className="material-symbols-outlined">close</span>
        <span>Cancel</span>
      </button>
    ) : (
      <button
        onClick={handleRunRules}
        className="w-full bg-primary text-white py-3 rounded-lg flex items-center justify-center gap-2 hover:bg-primary-container transition-all font-semibold shadow-lg shadow-primary/20"
      >
        <span className="material-symbols-outlined"
          style={{ fontVariationSettings: "'FILL' 1" }}>
          auto_awesome
        </span>
        <span>Run All Rules</span>
      </button>
    )}
  </div>
</section>
```

--- RIGHT COLUMN: RESULTS AREA (idle state) ---

When runStatus === 'idle', show empty state from Stitch HTML:

```tsx
<section className="bg-surface-container-lowest rounded-xl border border-dashed border-outline-variant p-xl flex flex-col items-center text-center justify-center min-h-[240px]">
  <div className="w-16 h-16 rounded-full bg-surface-container flex items-center justify-center mb-md">
    <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-outline text-[32px]">
      auto_awesome
    </span>
  </div>
  <h4 className="text-body font-semibold text-on-surface mb-xs">No results yet</h4>
  <p className="text-meta text-on-surface-variant max-w-[200px]">
    Run your rules to see which emails were moved and where.

</section>

--- handleRunRules STUB ---

```tsx
const handleRunRules = async () => {
  const activeRules = rules.filter(r => r.enabled)
  if (activeRules.length === 0) return

  setRunStatus('processing')
  setResults([])

  try {
    const { data } = await client.post('/rules/run', {
      start_date: startDate,
      end_date:   endDate,
      rules:      activeRules.map(r => ({
        id:          r.id,
        name:        r.name,
        instruction: r.instruction,
      })),
    })
    setResults(data.results)
    setRunStatus('done')
  } catch (e) {
    setRunStatus('idle')
    console.error('Run failed:', e)
  }
}
```

Import client from '../api/client' at top of file.

--- DELETE CONFIRM INLINE ---

Show inline confirmation when showDeleteConfirm is set.
Replace the matching rule row's actions section with:

```tsx
{showDeleteConfirm === rule.id ? (
  <div className="flex items-center gap-2 shrink-0">
    <span className="text-meta text-on-surface-variant">Delete?</span>
    <button
      onClick={() => setShowDeleteConfirm(null)}
      className="h-8 px-3 border border-outline-variant rounded text-meta text-on-surface-variant hover:bg-surface-container"
    >
      No
    </button>
    <button
      onClick={() => {
        setRules(prev => prev.filter(r => r.id !== rule.id))
        setShowDeleteConfirm(null)
      }}
      className="h-8 px-3 bg-error text-white rounded text-meta hover:bg-error/90"
    >
      Yes, delete
    </button>
  </div>
) : (
  // normal hover actions
)}
```


# ============================================================
# R-F3 — Rules.tsx: processing + results states
# ============================================================

Open src/pages/Rules.tsx.
Find the right column results area (currently only shows idle state).
Replace it with a three-state conditional based on runStatus.

--- PROCESSING STATE ---

When runStatus === 'processing', replace results area with:

```tsx
<section className="bg-surface-container-lowest rounded-xl border border-outline-variant p-lg shadow-sm space-y-4">

  {/* Header row */}
  <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
    <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
      <div className="w-2.5 h-2.5 rounded-full bg-primary animate-pulse" />
      <p className="text-body font-semibold text-on-surface">
        AI is reading your emails...
      </p>
    </div>
    <p className="text-meta text-outline">just started</p>
  </div>

  {/* Steps */}
  <div className="space-y-2">
    {[
      { label: 'Fetching emails from mailbox',        status: 'done',    detail: '47 emails'      },
      { label: 'Filtering by date range',             status: 'done',    detail: '32 in range'    },
      { label: `Sending ${rules.filter(r=>r.enabled).length} rules to AI...`, status: 'active', detail: 'In progress' },
      { label: 'Moving emails to folders',            status: 'pending', detail: '—'              },
    ].map((step, i) => (
      <div
        key={i}
        className={`flex items-center gap-3 px-3 py-2.5 rounded-lg ${
          step.status === 'active' ? 'bg-primary/5' : ''
        } ${step.status === 'pending' ? 'opacity-40' : ''}`}
      >
        {step.status === 'done' && (
          <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-emerald-500 text-[18px] shrink-0"
            style={{ fontVariationSettings: "'FILL' 1" }}>
            check_circle
          </span>
        )}
        {step.status === 'active' && (
          <div className="w-4 h-4 rounded-full border-2 border-primary border-t-transparent animate-spin shrink-0" />
        )}
        {step.status === 'pending' && (
          <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-outline text-[18px] shrink-0">
            radio_button_unchecked
          </span>
        )}
        <span className={`text-body flex-1 ${
          step.status === 'active' ? 'font-medium text-primary' :
          step.status === 'done'   ? 'text-on-surface' : 'text-on-surface-variant'
        }`}>
          {step.label}
        </span>
        <span className={`text-meta ml-auto shrink-0 ${
          step.status === 'active' ? 'text-primary' : 'text-outline'
        }`}>
          {step.detail}
        </span>
      </div>
    ))}
  </div>

  {/* What AI sees — collapsible */}
  <div className="bg-surface-container-low rounded-xl border border-outline-variant px-4 py-3">
    <p className="text-meta text-on-surface-variant font-medium flex items-center gap-2">
      <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-[16px] text-outline">visibility</span>
      What the AI is seeing
    </p>
    <div className="mt-3 space-y-2">
      <p className="text-meta text-outline">
        {rules.filter(r => r.enabled).length} active rules:
      </p>
      {rules.filter(r => r.enabled).map((rule, i) => (
        <div key={rule.id} className="flex items-start gap-2">
          <span className="w-5 h-5 rounded-full bg-primary/10 text-primary text-[10px] font-bold flex items-center justify-center shrink-0 mt-0.5">
            {i + 1}
          </span>
          <p className="text-meta text-on-surface-variant truncate">{rule.instruction}</p>
        </div>
      ))}
      <div className="border-t border-outline-variant pt-2 mt-2">
        <p className="text-meta text-on-surface-variant">
          Emails: {startDate} to {endDate}
        </p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

</section>
```

--- RESULTS STATE ---

When runStatus === 'done', replace results area with:

```tsx
<section className="bg-surface-container-lowest rounded-xl border border-outline-variant overflow-hidden shadow-sm">

  {/* Results header */}
  <div className="px-lg py-4 border-b border-outline-variant flex items-center justify-between">
    <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
      <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-emerald-500 text-[20px]"
        style={{ fontVariationSettings: "'FILL' 1" }}>
        check_circle
      </span>
      <p className="text-body font-semibold text-on-surface">
        Done · {results.length} emails processed
      </p>
    </div>
    <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
      {/* Summary chips */}
      <span className="bg-emerald-100 text-emerald-800 text-xs font-semibold px-2.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">
        {results.filter(r => r.status === 'moved').length} moved
      </span>
      {results.some(r => r.status === 'no_match') && (
        <span className="bg-surface-container text-on-surface-variant text-xs font-semibold px-2.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">
          {results.filter(r => r.status === 'no_match').length} no match
        </span>
      )}
      {results.some(r => r.status === 'error') && (
        <span className="bg-error-container text-error text-xs font-semibold px-2.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">
          {results.filter(r => r.status === 'error').length} error
        </span>
      )}
    </div>
  </div>

  {/* Filter pills */}
  <div className="px-lg py-3 bg-surface-container-low/40 border-b border-outline-variant flex gap-2 flex-wrap">
    {(['all', 'moved', 'no_match', 'error'] as const).map(f => (
      <button
        key={f}
        onClick={() => setResultFilter(f)}
        className={`text-meta font-meta font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full transition-colors ${
          resultFilter === f
            ? 'bg-primary text-on-primary'
            : 'bg-surface-container text-on-surface-variant border border-outline-variant/50 hover:bg-surface-container-high'
        }`}
      >
        {f === 'all'      ? `All ${results.length}` :
         f === 'moved'    ? `Moved ${results.filter(r=>r.status==='moved').length}` :
         f === 'no_match' ? `No match ${results.filter(r=>r.status==='no_match').length}` :
                            `Error ${results.filter(r=>r.status==='error').length}`}
      </button>
    ))}
  </div>

  {/* Results list */}
  <div className="divide-y divide-outline-variant max-h-[400px] overflow-y-auto">
    {results
      .filter(r => resultFilter === 'all' || r.status === resultFilter)
      .map(result => (
        <div
          key={result.emailId}
          className={`px-lg py-3 flex items-start gap-3 text-body border-l-4 ${
            result.status === 'moved' && result.confidence === 'high'   ? 'border-l-emerald-400' :
            result.status === 'moved' && result.confidence === 'medium' ? 'border-l-amber-400'   :
            result.status === 'error'    ? 'border-l-error bg-error-container/20'                :
            result.status === 'no_match' ? 'border-l-outline-variant bg-surface-container/20'    :
            'border-l-emerald-400'
          }`}
        >
          {/* Content */}
          <div className="flex-1 min-w-0">
            {/* Subject */}
            <p className="text-body font-medium text-on-surface truncate">
              {result.subject}
            </p>
            {/* From */}
            <p className="text-meta text-on-surface-variant mt-0.5">{result.from}</p>
            {/* Rule matched + folder */}
            {result.status === 'moved' && (
              <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mt-1 flex-wrap">
                <span className="text-meta text-outline">Rule:</span>
                <span className="text-meta text-on-surface-variant font-medium">
                  {result.matchedRuleName}
                </span>
                <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-primary text-[14px]">
                  arrow_forward
                </span>
                <span className="text-mono font-mono bg-surface-container-high text-on-surface-variant px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-xs">
                  {result.folder}
                </span>
              </div>
            )}
            {result.status === 'no_match' && (
              <p className="text-meta text-on-surface-variant italic mt-1">
                No rule matched
              </p>
            )}
            {result.status === 'error' && (
              <p className="text-meta text-error mt-1">{result.errorMessage}</p>
            )}
          </div>

          {/* Reasoning icon */}
          {result.status === 'moved' && (
            <button
              onClick={() => setShowReasoningFor(
                showReasoningFor === result.emailId ? null : result.emailId
              )}
              className="p-1 text-outline hover:text-primary transition-colors shrink-0 mt-0.5"
              title="View AI reasoning"
            >
              <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-[16px]">chat_bubble_outline</span>
            </button>
          )}
        </div>
      ))
    }

    {results.filter(r => resultFilter === 'all' || r.status === resultFilter).length === 0 && (
      <div className="px-lg py-8 text-center text-meta text-outline">
        No results in this category
      </div>
    )}
  </div>

</section>
```

--- REASONING DRAWER ---

Add a small inline reasoning block below the matched result row
when showReasoningFor === result.emailId:

```tsx
{showReasoningFor === result.emailId && (
  <div className="mx-lg mb-3 px-4 py-3 bg-surface-container-low rounded-lg border border-outline-variant">
    <p className="text-meta text-on-surface-variant font-medium mb-1 flex items-center gap-1">
      <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-[14px]">auto_awesome</span>
      AI reasoning
    </p>
    <p className="text-meta text-on-surface-variant leading-relaxed">
      {result.reasoning}
    </p>
  </div>
)}
```

This reasoning block is placed INSIDE the results list,
immediately after the row it belongs to.

--- EDIT RULE MODAL ---

When editingRule is not null, show a simple centered modal
(same overlay pattern as TaskViewModal):

```tsx
{editingRule && (
  <>
    <div className="fixed inset-0 z-40 bg-black/40 backdrop-blur-[2px]"
      onClick={() => setEditingRule(null)} />
    <div className="fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center p-4">
      <div className="w-full max-w-[520px] bg-[#F4F5F7] rounded-2xl shadow-2xl overflow-hidden">
        <div className="h-16 bg-white px-8 border-b border-outline-variant flex items-center justify-between">
          <h3 className="font-page-title text-page-title text-on-surface">Edit Rule</h3>
          <button onClick={() => setEditingRule(null)}
            className="w-8 h-8 flex items-center justify-center text-secondary hover:bg-surface-container-high rounded-full">
            <span className="material-symbols-outlined">close</span>
          </button>
        </div>
        <div className="p-8 space-y-4 bg-white mx-8 my-6 rounded-xl border border-outline-variant shadow-sm">
          <div>
            <label className="block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1.5">Rule name</label>
            <input type="text" defaultValue={editingRule.name}
              className="w-full h-9 rounded-lg border border-gray-300 bg-white px-3 text-body focus:border-primary focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/20 focus:outline-none" />
          </div>
          <div>
            <label className="block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1.5">Instruction</label>
            <textarea rows={4} defaultValue={editingRule.instruction}
              className="w-full rounded-lg border border-gray-300 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-body focus:border-primary focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/20 focus:outline-none resize-none" />
          </div>
        </div>
        <div className="h-16 bg-white px-8 border-t border-outline-variant flex items-center justify-end gap-3">
          <button onClick={() => setEditingRule(null)}
            className="h-9 px-4 border border-outline-variant bg-white text-on-surface-variant rounded-lg text-body font-medium hover:bg-surface-container">
            Cancel
          </button>
          <button onClick={() => setEditingRule(null)}
            className="h-9 px-6 bg-primary text-white rounded-lg text-body font-medium shadow-sm hover:bg-primary-container">
            Save Changes
          </button>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </>
)}
```

After writing, verify:
  ✓ Idle: rule list + empty state in right column
  ✓ Processing: run panel disabled + steps card + what AI sees
  ✓ Done: results list with filter pills + reasoning inline
  ✓ Edit modal opens/closes correctly
  ✓ Delete inline confirmation works
  ✓ Add rule form shows/hides under the card header


# ============================================================
# R-B1 — Backend: rules router + LLM service
# ============================================================

Step 1 — Create app/services/rules_service.py

```python
"""
Rules Service — sends email data + plain English rules to LLM.
The LLM reads all rules at once and decides which folder
each email belongs to, returning structured JSON.
"""
import json
import logging
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional
import httpx

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
GRAPH_PROXY_BASE = 'https://o365-graph-proxy-ms6.gaiacloud.jpmchase.net'


def _build_system_prompt(rules: list[dict]) -> str:
    """
    Build the LLM system prompt that includes all active rules.
    Each rule is a plain English instruction from the user.
    """
    rules_text = '\n'.join(
        f"{i+1}. [{r['name']}] {r['instruction']}"
        for i, r in enumerate(rules)
    )
    return f"""You are an email organiser. You have the following rules to apply:

{rules_text}

For each email I give you, decide which rule applies and which folder
the email should be moved to based on the matching rule.

IMPORTANT:
- Apply the FIRST matching rule only. Rules are checked in order.
- If no rule matches, set status to "no_match" and leave folder empty.
- Be concise in your reasoning — one or two sentences maximum.
- Extract the folder name from the rule instruction itself.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON array. No markdown, no preamble.
Each element must have these exact fields:
  email_id, rule_id, rule_name, folder, confidence, reasoning, status

confidence must be one of: "high", "medium", "low"
status must be one of: "moved", "no_match", "error"

Example response format:
[
  {{
    "email_id": "abc123",
    "rule_id": "1",
    "rule_name": "SABS Team",
    "folder": "SABS Team",
    "confidence": "high",
    "reasoning": "Email is from Sarah who is listed in the SABS team rule.",
    "status": "moved"
  }}
]"""


def _build_user_message(emails: list[dict]) -> str:
    """Format emails for the LLM prompt."""
    lines = []
    for email in emails:
        lines.append(
            f"EMAIL ID: {email['id']}\n"
            f"From: {email.get('sender_name', '')} <{email.get('sender_email', '')}>\n"
            f"Subject: {email.get('subject', '')}\n"
            f"Preview: {email.get('preview', '')[:300]}\n"
            f"---"
        )
    return '\n'.join(lines)


def run_rules(
    rules: list[dict],
    emails: list[dict],
    model: str = 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514',
) -> list[dict]:
    """
    Send all rules + all emails to the LLM in one call.
    Returns a list of decisions, one per email.

    Args:
        rules:  list of {id, name, instruction} dicts
        emails: list of email dicts from mailbox_service
        model:  Anthropic model string

    Returns:
        list of {email_id, rule_id, rule_name, folder,
                 confidence, reasoning, status} dicts
    """
    if not rules:
        raise ValueError("No active rules provided")
    if not emails:
        return []

    system_prompt = _build_system_prompt(rules)
    user_message  = _build_user_message(emails)

    logger.info(
        f"Sending {len(emails)} emails + {len(rules)} rules to LLM ({model})"
    )

    # Call Anthropic API directly — same endpoint as artifacts
    response = httpx.post(
        'https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages',
        headers={
            'Content-Type':         'application/json',
            'anthropic-version':    '2023-06-01',
        },
        json={
            'model':      model,
            'max_tokens': 4096,
            'system':     system_prompt,
            'messages': [
                {'role': 'user', 'content': user_message}
            ],
        },
        timeout=120.0,
    )
    response.raise_for_status()

    content = response.json()['content'][0]['text'].strip()

    # Strip markdown fences if present
    if content.startswith('```'):
        content = content.split('\n', 1)[1].rsplit('```', 1)[0].strip()

    decisions = json.loads(content)
    logger.info(f"LLM returned {len(decisions)} decisions")
    return decisions
```

Step 2 — Create app/models/rule.py

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional, List

class RuleDefinition(BaseModel):
    id: str
    name: str
    instruction: str    # plain English

class RunRulesRequest(BaseModel):
    start_date: str     # YYYY-MM-DD
    end_date: str       # YYYY-MM-DD
    rules: List[RuleDefinition]
    is_shared: bool = False
    mailbox: str = ''
    model: str = 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514'

class RuleDecision(BaseModel):
    email_id: str
    subject: str
    from_: str
    rule_id: str
    rule_name: str
    folder: str
    confidence: str     # high / medium / low
    reasoning: str
    status: str         # moved / no_match / error
    error_message: Optional[str] = None
```

Step 3 — Create app/routers/rules.py

```python
import logging
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
from app.models.rule import RunRulesRequest, RuleDecision
from app.services.rules_service import run_rules
from app.services.mailbox_service import get_emails
from app.config import settings

router = APIRouter(prefix='/rules', tags=['rules'])
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@router.post('/run')
def run_rules_endpoint(body: RunRulesRequest):
    """
    Main endpoint — fetch emails for date range, send all
    rules to LLM together, return decisions.

    The frontend sends the full rule list (only enabled ones).
    This endpoint:
      1. Fetches emails from the mailbox for the date range
      2. Sends ALL emails + ALL rules to LLM in one call
      3. For each 'moved' decision, moves the email to the folder
      4. Returns full results for the UI to display
    """
    if not body.rules:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='No rules provided')

    # Step 1: fetch emails
    logger.info(
        f"Fetching emails from {body.start_date} to {body.end_date}"
    )
    try:
        mailbox = body.mailbox or settings.PERSONAL_MAILBOX
        all_emails = get_emails(
            is_shared=body.is_shared,
            mailbox=mailbox,
            filter_type='all',
            limit=200,
        )
    except Exception as e:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=503,
            detail=f"Cannot fetch emails: {str(e)}"
        )

    # Filter by date range
    try:
        start_dt = datetime.strptime(body.start_date, '%Y-%m-%d')
        end_dt   = datetime.strptime(body.end_date,   '%Y-%m-%d') + timedelta(days=1)
    except ValueError:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=400,
            detail='Invalid date format. Use YYYY-MM-DD'
        )

    filtered_emails = [
        e for e in all_emails
        if e.get('timestamp')
        and start_dt.isoformat() <= e['timestamp'] <= end_dt.isoformat()
    ]

    logger.info(
        f"{len(all_emails)} total emails → {len(filtered_emails)} in date range"
    )

    if not filtered_emails:
        return {
            'total_emails':    0,
            'emails_in_range': 0,
            'results':         [],
            'run_at':          datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        }

    # Step 2: send to LLM
    rules_dicts = [r.dict() for r in body.rules]
    try:
        decisions = run_rules(
            rules=rules_dicts,
            emails=filtered_emails,
            model=body.model,
        )
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"LLM call failed: {e}")
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=500,
            detail=f"LLM processing failed: {str(e)}"
        )

    # Step 3: build results and move emails
    # Build email lookup for enriching results
    email_map = {e['id']: e for e in filtered_emails}

    results = []
    for decision in decisions:
        email_id = decision.get('email_id', '')
        email    = email_map.get(email_id, {})
        status   = decision.get('status', 'no_match')

        # Attempt to move the email if status is 'moved'
        error_msg = None
        if status == 'moved' and decision.get('folder'):
            try:
                from app.services.mailbox_service import move_email
                move_email(
                    is_shared=body.is_shared,
                    mailbox=mailbox,
                    email_id=email_id,
                    destination_folder=decision['folder'],
                )
            except Exception as e:
                error_msg = str(e)
                status = 'error'
                logger.warning(f"Failed to move email {email_id}: {e}")

        results.append({
            'emailId':         email_id,
            'subject':         email.get('subject', ''),
            'from':            email.get('sender_email', ''),
            'matchedRuleId':   decision.get('rule_id', ''),
            'matchedRuleName': decision.get('rule_name', ''),
            'folder':          decision.get('folder', ''),
            'confidence':      decision.get('confidence', 'low'),
            'reasoning':       decision.get('reasoning', ''),
            'status':          status,
            'errorMessage':    error_msg,
        })

    moved   = sum(1 for r in results if r['status'] == 'moved')
    no_match= sum(1 for r in results if r['status'] == 'no_match')
    errors  = sum(1 for r in results if r['status'] == 'error')

    logger.info(
        f"Run complete: {moved} moved, {no_match} no match, {errors} errors"
    )

    return {
        'total_emails':    len(all_emails),
        'emails_in_range': len(filtered_emails),
        'moved':           moved,
        'no_match':        no_match,
        'errors':          errors,
        'results':         results,
        'run_at':          datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
    }
```

Step 4 — Register the router in app/main.py:

```python
from app.routers.rules import router as rules_router
app.include_router(rules_router, prefix='/api')
```

Step 5 — Add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to app/config.py and .env:

In config.py:
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: str = ''

In rules_service.py, add the key to the request headers:
  'x-api-key': settings.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY,

In .env:
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key_here

In .env.example:
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_anthropic_api_key_here

Step 6 — Test with curl:

  curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/rules/run \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
      "start_date": "2025-05-01",
      "end_date": "2025-05-14",
      "rules": [
        {
          "id": "1",
          "name": "SABS Team",
          "instruction": "If emails are from anyone in the SABS team, move to SABS Team folder."
        }
      ],
      "is_shared": false
    }'

  Expected response shape:
  {
    "total_emails": 47,
    "emails_in_range": 32,
    "moved": 6,
    "no_match": 25,
    "errors": 1,
    "results": [...],
    "run_at": "2025-05-14T14:32:00"
  }
